channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Acclaimed 20th century philosopher Jürgen Habermas dies at 96
item.description: Associated with the Frankfurt School, Habermas was a world-renowned thinker on modernity and democracy who helped shape German post-war and post-reunification political discourse.
item.content:encoded:
Associated with the Frankfurt School, Habermas was a world-renowned thinker on modernity and democracy who helped shape German post-war and post-reunification political discourse.

(Image credit: Louisa Gouliamaki)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: 'War of the Worlds' remake sinks to the bottom at this year's Razzie Awards
item.description: The surveillance industry version of HG Wells' 1898 classic sci-fi novel stars Ice Cube, and won accolades for worst picture, actor, director and more.
item.content:encoded:
The surveillance industry version of HG Wells' 1898 classic sci-fi novel stars Ice Cube, and won accolades for worst picture, actor, director and more.

(Image credit: Amazon Prime Video)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for March 14, 2026: With Not My Job guest John Cusack
item.description: This week's show was recorded in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, guest judge and scorekeeper Alzo Slade, Not My Job guest John Cusack and panelists Rachel Coster, Adam Felber, and Joyelle Nicole Johnson. Click the audio link above to hear the whole show.
item.content:encoded:
This week's show was recorded in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, guest judge and scorekeeper Alzo Slade, Not My Job guest John Cusack and panelists Rachel Coster, Adam Felber, and Joyelle Nicole Johnson. Click the audio link above to hear the whole show.

(Image credit: Frazer Harrison)

item.title: Best picture nominees are leaving theaters sooner than they used to
item.description: Best picture nominees are spending less time in wide release. But indie theater owners say Oscar season is still a draw.
item.content:encoded: Best picture nominees are spending less time in wide release. But indie theater owners say Oscar season is still a draw.
item.dc:creator: Rahul Mukherjee

item.title: The Oscars are Sunday night. Here's how to watch
item.description: The Oscars start at 7 p.m. Eastern, 4 p.m. Pacific on Sunday, airing on ABC and streaming on Hulu.
item.content:encoded:
The Oscars start at 7 p.m. Eastern, 4 p.m. Pacific on Sunday, airing on ABC and streaming on Hulu.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck

item.title: Reframing Georgia O'Keeffe's legacy and protecting the land she loved
item.description: Georgia O'Keeffe called the New Mexico high desert "my country," but Pueblo peoples predated her. A more complex view is emerging amid efforts to preserve the land.
item.content:encoded:
Georgia O'Keeffe called the New Mexico high desert "my country," but Pueblo peoples predated her. A more complex view is emerging amid efforts to preserve the land.

(Image credit: Minesh Bacrania for NPR)
item.dc:creator: John Burnett

item.title: Kennedy Center president departs – months before the art complex's scheduled closing
item.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump announced Friday afternoon that Richard Grenell is leaving the Kennedy Center. The arts complex is scheduled to close in July for renovations.
item.content:encoded:
Melania at the Kennedy Center in Washington, DC, on January 29, 2026.'/>

In a post on Truth Social, President Trump announced Friday afternoon that Richard Grenell is leaving the Kennedy Center. The arts complex is scheduled to close in July for renovations.

(Image credit: ALEX WROBLEWSKI/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Some Gen Z Americans can't stop 'Chinamaxxing'
item.description: Despite rising tensions between the world's two largest economies, a growing number of young Americans are becoming captivated by China, as seen in the online trend "Chinamaxxing."
item.content:encoded:
Despite rising tensions between the world's two largest economies, a growing number of young Americans are becoming captivated by China, as seen in the online trend "Chinamaxxing."

(Image credit: Adek Berry)
item.dc:creator: Ashish Valentine

item.title: And the Oscar goes to — wait, why is it called an Oscar?
item.description: The Academy Awards officially adopted the "Oscars" nickname in 1939. But who is Oscar, and who started calling them that? We may never know. But here are four enduring legends to consider.
item.content:encoded:
The Academy Awards officially adopted the "Oscars" nickname in 1939. But who is Oscar, and who started calling them that? We may never know. But here are four enduring legends to consider.

(Image credit: Matt Sayles)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 'Scarpetta' is a captivating murder mystery — and a high-wire balancing act
item.description: Based on a series of novels by best-selling author Patricia Cornwell, Scarpetta follows two different mysteries from two different timelines. It's structurally complicated — but it all holds up.
item.content:encoded: Based on a series of novels by best-selling author Patricia Cornwell, Scarpetta follows two different mysteries from two different timelines. It's structurally complicated — but it all holds up.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli
